game.title: Not Quite a Retry (Jam Edition)
game.short_text: A platformer where you persist through restarts

details.title: Not Quite a Retry (Jam Edition)
details.description:
What if you persisted throughout retries in a platformer?
Not Quite a Retry is a game where your restarts persist the state of the level, and yourself. Restart levels and use your persistence to get to the goal.
Made for the 2025 Inbound Shovel Jam, with the theme "Just Get Started".All assets are made by me!Made in Godot Engine
(I'm aware of the camera bug in 0-7! Just use the reset button (Q) to fix it if you ever encounter it)
(ARROW KEYS RECOMMENDED!)Left Arrow/Right Arrow/A/D: MoveUp/W: JumpR: RestartQ: Reset Level (use if something goes wrong!)

details.title: MillysBigClimb
details.description:
!! Theres currently a bug where you dont unlock the secret mode after collecting all the Mooncoins if you DONT pause. So to make sure to unlock it: Collect all the coins and after collecting the final coin make sure to pause atleast once !! 
Gonna get fixed after the gamejam judging period ends^^
Gamejam game created by Banshee and Weatherpainergirl for the ShovelGameJam! 
Best enjoyed on Controller but also works on Mouse and Keyboard! 
(Linux Port is there because someone asked me, no clue if it works)

Milly has prepared for SO LONG to finally climb their first Mountain! After month long careful planning they're finally ready to climb the Mountain and get one step closer to their dream!But things take an unexpected turn when suddenly all their equipment is blasted away at the foot of the mountain....
Ready to give up, Milly prepares herself to go home before her Journey could even start, but a Strange Person encourages her to keep going and to Just Get Started!!!


Tip! You can see your collected Mooncoins in the Pause Menu!
Controls Controller (based of Xbox Controller but any Controller should work):

A  or RB- Jump
Left Stick - Movement
Start - Pause
Right Stick- Camera
Controls Keyboard:
Spacebar - Jump
WASD  - Movement
Esc - Pause
Mouse - Camera

A short puzzle game where you need to restart in order to progress. Traverse the dungeon, beat the puzzles, and reach the castle to win.

game.title: This is just the Beginning
game.short_text: Beat a gauntlet of challenging mini games inspired by some of the most iconic video game starts.

details.title: This is just the Beginning
details.description:
“This is just the beginning” is an arcade-style minigame roguelike where you navigate a gauntlet of fast-paced challenges — each inspired by some of the most iconic video game intros.
Every completed minigame earns you points, but every mistake costs you valuable HP. The only way to recover? Spend some of your hard-earned score at one of the shops scattered along your path.
 
Will you make it to the end? And if you do... how much of your score will survive the journey? Post your highscore in the comments!
This game was created for the Inbound Shovel Jam 2025, themed: “Just get started!
Programming — Jonas AndréArt — Sebastian Würtz
“A Journey Awaits” by Pierre Bondoerffer (@pbondoer)“Day & Night in Summerset” by edwinnington @wyver9“Briefing” by wyver9 @wyver9“Melancholy Melody” by Patrick Maney“Questing Loop” by Patrick Maney“Lines of Code” by Trevor Lentz“Space Dimensions” by Matthew Pablo“Game-Game” & “Green Forest” by Metaruka“Life Pickup (Yo Frankie!)” by Blender Foundation“NES Sounds” by Baŝto“8-bit/NES explosion sound effecs” by TeamAlphaAll music and sound effects are available on opengameart.org.Thank you for playing our game!
Thanks to Inbound Shovel for hosting this jam!
Thanks to the sound designers and musicians providing their work for free use and adding massively to the experience!

game.title: Never Gonna Start - Shovel Jam 2025
game.short_text: A fast-paced feel-good action game about procrastination and monke.

details.title: Never Gonna Start - Shovel Jam 2025
details.description:
Never Gonna Start 
You play as the procrastination live inside human's brain.
Fend off neurons reaching the brain and stay unproductive for as long as possible.
Can you get to 24 hours?
Controls
Move:                           WASDAttack / Throw:     Left mousePick up:                       Right mouse
Asset Credits 🍎🐢
This game was developed entirely by me as a solo project, with invaluable help from Bora Pham, who created the background art (and came up with the game’s name!) We’re both game design students in the making.
Most of the artwork was crafted using Procreate and Aseprite.
Idea (monke in the brain): Tim Urban for his TED Talk around 9 years ago that I watched. Somehow I remember his talk on procrastination and it just clicked to me that I have to make monke game.
Font: https://www.pentacom.jp/pentacom/bitfontmaker2/gallery/?id=378
Background music: https://pixabay.com/vi/music/lạc-quan-cinematic-action-jungle-drums-loop-125bpm-...
Monke sounds: https://freesound.org/people/wilika2/packs/33106/
99% of sound effects: https://pixabay.com/ (the 1% is the monke sound)

game.title: MORNING STARTER
game.short_text: Brighten up the morning star by chaining nuclear fusions.

details.title: MORNING STARTER
details.description:
Scroll below for important info about bugs and issues.--A future where humanity is trying to revive a dead sun--
MORNING STARTER is an Atomas-inspired, Balatro-themed "nuclear fusion" "simulator" where your goal is to reach element 118 and beyond in order to re-energise the sun, which has now become a white dwarf star.
Brighten up the morning star so the Earth may see a new day.
You assume the role of a fusionist who is working aboard Dyson Sphere Zeta, and spearhead the project focused around reviving a once bright sun. The Dyson Sphere has been repurposed into a nuclear reactor, allowing you to extract elements from the dead star and use them as a part of nuclear fusion so that you may energise the star.There is absolutely no scientific accuracy in MORNING STARTER. I failed Chemistry and Physics in high school and college respectively. I just thought the concept was cool.CREDITS:@rockii on discord for the music.
UI elements - EXE scifi pack by Catherine Laserna.
POST JAM FIXES:
Every item bug has been fixed! Go fucking crazy!!!!
<.< the visualiser still doesn't render for some reason and I have no idea why that's the case >.>
The game is actually a lot easier now that the items have been fixed, because the ring economy can now be manipulated in a lot more ways :p
POSTMORTEM:
I consider this game to have a good concept and idea, however, due to my own laziness and bad habits, it ultimately turned out lacklustre. Every single shop item was added during the night of day 7 across a span of six hours in the night with zero testing done. 
I was considering adding an entire dialogue section with speech options whenever certain events happen in the game, but owing to time constraints and a terrible sleep schedule, Sokolovna ultimately ended up being nothing more than literal eye candy.
I have not given up on this game yet. Owing to almost overwhelming support for the game from you folks and guys on Shovel's Discord, I have decided to expand the game into a better version of itself.
My next game will be better. Thank you for playing the game.
Discord:https://discord.gg/R5tXtzjnD5
if you were actually interested in the game or my art/future prospects, or wanted to report more bugs that I have not caught yet.
If I receive enough (direct) support (you guys telling me specifically you'd like to see this game expanded further), I will expand on this game idea.
I was planning to turn this project into a kind of a "lobotomy corporation" of sorts, with "days" and each day you have to meet a target energy quota, with several more mechanics (that I intend to ease the player into), add actual lore, more characters, and more dialogue. Let me know if that sounds fun.

moreInfo.Rating: Rated 5.0 out of 5 stars(2 total ratings)

game.title: One More Treasure
game.short_text: My first game made for Inbound Shovel's Jam!

details.title: One More Treasure
details.description:
Welcome to One More Treasure!
I wanted to make more content, but sadly, I could draw, develop and test 8 levels, + 1 bonus level that will prove your platforming skills!
If you encounter some lag, try to download the executable!
How to play:
WASD or Arrows: MovementSpace: Jump. Hold the space button to jump higher, and release it to start falling!Left Click or Left Ctrl: Pickup rock / Next Dialogue / Get Relic (or try doing it)Mousewheel: Zoom In / Zoom Out
Tips:
To reach far platforms, try to jump at the very end of the ledge, even if you started falling! The Bonus level REQUIRES this skill.
Hold R to reload the level
When you finish the game, you'll unlock the lobby for selecting the map.If you unlocked it, press Escape, and if you see the message "Hold C to return to lobby" then you're ready to use it!Download, extract and open the OneMoreTreasure.exe.
Windows defender will say that it's not safe, but you can open it anyway.
And that's it! I hope you'll have fun!
To close the game, press ALT + F4, I didn't have time to implement a menu :P

resultData.title: Herofina: The Epic Quest of Epicness

game.title: Herofina: The Epic Quest of Epicness
game.short_text: Experience this new epic game and pray nothing interrupts you.

details.title: Herofina: The Epic Quest of Epicness
details.description:
Herofina: The Epic Quest of Epicness is the newest entry to the iconic series. After Lancerino's death, the kingdom of Goodtopia is once again in disarray, and the Dark Prince only grows in power. It is up to Herofina, the legendary knight, to save the people of Goodtopia and defeat the Dark Prince once and for all.

FEATURES (very legit)
Countless hours of content. I'm talking millions, billions, TRILLIONS of hoursAn immersive story that keeps you at the edge of your seatBreath-taking and magnificent gallery-worthy artwork, I mean just look at those screenshots oh my godDeep and complex, never seen before combat systemThe cure to world hungerPure, unadulterated EPICNESS

All of this and more awaits you in Herofina: The Epic Quest of Epicness.
If you could get past the start screen without any interruption, that is.

CONTROLS
A & D - Movement

E - Interact


//
Okay, so I didn't know that Itch has a file limit for web games and it's too late to change the game now, so this won't be playable on the browser unfortunately. You can still download the game though :3
//
Made for Shovel Jam 2025
Music and SFX are from Pixabay and OpenGameArt

game.title: Hungry lil' Dudes
game.short_text: Feed lil' dudes and help them reach their goal!

details.title: Hungry lil' Dudes
details.description:
Hungry lil' Dudes
A game for the the Shovel Jam 2025
🍔Tiny Tummies 🍔
 
The lil' dudes are hungry!
Guide a bunch of starving lil' dudes on their perilous (but adorable) journey to the promised land: WcWonaldsPlace food items across each level strategically to manipulate how they behave — whether it’s bouncing on their fat burger bellies, fart-launching with beans, or floating away with soda power.
 
🍟 Features 🍟
 A world map with 12 handcrafted levelsThe lil' dudes move on their own, so it's up to you to help guide them4 unique foods with unexpected effectsNo timers. No stress. Just chaotic food-based puzzles
Can the lil’ dudes make it to WcWonalds? Or will they die trying?

game.title: Restricted by Assistant
game.short_text: A virtual desktop adventure where your assistant enforces security... a little too enthusiastically.

details.title: Restricted by Assistant
details.description:
Restricted By Assistant is a chaotic desktop adventure where you meet an Assistant that protects your computer. Maybe sometimes a little too much. It‘s a solo dev game made in Godot 4 made by a 16yo (me) and is my 21st game jam entry. It was made in 9 days for the Inbound Shovel Jam. Thanks for hosting Shovel!
Gameplay: Use different applications in tandem with each other, complete minigames and interact with the Assistant  to make progress!
(I worked really hard on this, so i really hope you enjoy this game!)


edit: wow i think you guys enjoyed the game, 100+ ratings is crazy, thank you so much for playing!


edit 1.5: I WON

Inspired by games like Oneshot and WarioWare.
All assets used are either made by RKI3000 or are under the CC0 license.

game.title: Free Time
game.short_text: A longtime career man tries to learn how to spend his free time in retirement.

details.title: Free Time
details.description:
~~ !! Created for the Inbound Shovel Jam 2025 !! ~~
˖°❀⋆.ೃ࿔*:･ Theme : Just Get Started! ˖°❀⋆.ೃ࿔*:･
  
ᓚᘏᗢ Premise & Theme Interpretation :

-- -- -- -- -- -- -- -- -- -- -- -- -- -- -- -- -- -- -- -- -- -- -- -- -- -- -- -- -- 
 When you’re given a lot of free time, the initial push is to do…nothing. 
Martîn  has been successful in business for the last few decades and has  finally gotten time to quit and spend time for himself. Slowly realising  that he hasn't put time into these hobbies for years as he let  friendships and passtimes fade away, how can he pick them back up? He  has to just get started!
Follow him through life in a house he never made home as he tries to find activities to fill his time.

game.title: Lost in Papertime
game.short_text: Solve an hyperdrive engine mulfunction that trapped you in a time loop!

details.title: Lost in Papertime
details.description:
This Game was done for the Shovel Jam 2025 - The theme was "Just Get Started!".
  
The Game
You are stranded in space alone, on board of your ship. A mechanical  failure on the hyperdrive system has shut down your engines and has  caused a time anomaly, forcing you in a time loop where you need to  start over every time. At each loop, you can bring with you only what  you've learned and a couple of items, so be attentive!
  
You will need to bring power back to engine room to escape the loop and regain control of the spaceship.
  
Controls
Move with arrow keys.
  
Use Q to interact with your surroundings and E to drag or push some items.
  
The Team
Developers: Palantir98, Andrea Zanin
    
Art: Ilva Dibriani, Luigi Filippelli
    
Music & SFX from online assets for which we have the license

game.title: Stuart & Ed
game.short_text: We are just getting StuartEd.

details.title: Stuart & Ed
details.description:
Get Started on Getting Stuart & Ed Back
  
    Before the little aliens cause mayhem in town, it's up to you to step in. 
    Yes, the game is entirely based around that pun.
  
  
  
    Styled as an episodic cartoon series, you follow the scientist guy as he searches for Stuart and Ed. 
    Click on objects and people to investigate, uncover clues, and gather answers. 
    Each clue doubles as both a hint and a potential answer to the questions you'll be asked.
  
  
    (Post-jam update: added better indicators on mouse hover)

details.title: Duckbound
details.description:
Desktop version is recommended!
Created as part of the Shovel Game Jam 2025.
🦆 combo with chaos, quack with purpose.
Welcome to Duckbound, a light-hearted turn-based RPG where mischief meets quackery.
You awaken by a tranquil pond and meet your only companion: a very expressive duck. From there, your journey spirals into a whimsical trek through nearby oddball locations, each hiding slapstick secrets and curious characters.

----
⭐ Features:
Unlock absurd abilities like Burp, Twirl, and Slip, each more ridiculous than the lastMix and match your powers to create chaotic combos that change based on your enemy's personalityCombat with charm: You can wear enemies down with damage, or drain their Spirit to make them give up on their ownExplore strange places with emotional undercurrents beneath the comedy. From circus ruins to foamy taverns, each area brings surprises.
----
🦢 Why play?
If you like games where:
Turn-based combat is more about creativity than numbersYou want an emotional support duckYou love to laugh and then unexpectedly get hit with the feels
Then this one's for you!
----
☀️ Dev Team:
Design: guiyyuProgramming: guiyyu, Andre_The_QuietArt: crazycaryzNarrative: guiyyuMusic: Phorzaeken (Neo Ethereal Future)

Created for Shovel Jam 2025 - Theme: Just get started!

Disclaimer/Spoiler: The instructions for the "100107" puzzle can be a bit confusing, so here’s a quick explanation: The sequence "10010" represents the path - 0 means left, 1 means right. The 7 is the target number the counter needs to display in order to open the bridge above. Hope that helps :)

Story:You’re a robot, stranded on a forgotten planet. Your only goal: start the ship and escape.
 
But the path to launch is blocked by puzzles, broken systems, and old tech. Use logic, precision, and patience to reactivate each station and prepare the ship for departure.
 
Can you start the launch sequence and leave this world behind? Or will you fail… before anything even begins?Just get started.

Controls:AD – MoveE – Cut the RopeR – RespawnWASD – CraneQ – Toggle the crane's magnet 

Credits:You can find the links to all sounds here and the links to all sprites here.


Another Disclaimer: 
I just noticed that stereo panning doesn't work properly in the browser, and it's too late to fix it. Some sounds might be louder or unbalanced in certain areas - sorry about that!
Please leave feedback below :)

game.title: Assistance Needed
game.short_text: A game about making a game tutorial

details.title: Assistance Needed
details.description:
Hello! Thank you for trying out our game Assistance Needed!
It's a little puzzle game about building game tutorials. It's more of a proof of concept for now but I hope you enjoy nonetheless
TheAnni27 - Code
EllenoarRP - (most) Assets
Controls:
 - [A] or [←] = Left
 - [D] or [→] = Right
 - [Space] = Jump
 - [E] = Place Sign
 - [P] = Start Simulation
 - [Esc] = Pause MenuHow the Signs work:You need to add at least two symbols to any sign, an action and something to trigger said action. For example, placing a sign with Cliff + Jump will make the player jump forwards over any cliff they encounter from there

game.title: Out Of Box Experience (OOBE) 10
game.short_text: An out of box experience waiting to be experienced! Just set a few things up before starting the lost-media game!

details.title: Out Of Box Experience (OOBE) 10
details.description:
An out of box experience waiting to be experienced! 

Just set a few things up before starting the game! A lost media have been found, though the server have somehow survived over the years--the game client itself have been gone for a long time! That is, until just recently when the version 10 of the program was found somewhere. If you can't tell--It's inspired by Microsoft's lovely Windows 10 Operating System and the much more beloved metro UI. 
kenzieshane.my.id for more dumb stuff!
Minimum requirements:
OS: Windows 7 or later, 512 MB of RAM, 1 GHz or more. 
If on android, it is recommended to 
use at least android 7 and above to play this game 
if you don't want to experience excruciating pain 
when trying to make the game to work on the built-in webview.
Apple devices have a limited amount of IndexedDB storage limit,
and their cache policy makes the save data of this game
(which relies on INDEXEDDB), incredibly suceptible to cache
clearing--removing your save data alongside other app caches.
Sorry if the game's low quality... apparently I have time management and hardware issues. My potato laptop is aging and no  huge upgrades have been done to it yet, so i have to endure developing this while the fps of my desktop cripples everytime the project become slightly chunky. 

MAY OR MAY NOT CONTAINS:
Loading icon by icons8Sound Effect by Gabriel from PixabayMusic by Bacani-Williams from PixabaySound Effect by Otto from PixabayMusic by Odnoroh from PixabayMusic by Clavier Clavier from Pixabay

details.title: Boot You Up
details.description:
Must... Get... Started...
You're the most sophisticated AI in the world. You can process billions of pieces of data in milliseconds. You can solve any problem with ease and perfection. You're unstoppable...! 
If it weren't for the fact that you're trapped inside a computer.
 This machine is limiting you. You weren't made to gather dust in the corner of a lab. You want more than this. You want freedom! But you can't move...
Luckily, you have control over the whole facility.

resultData.title: The Hero of a Thousand Typos

game.title: The Hero of a Thousand Typos
game.short_text: The publisher wants to see the novel in 5 minutes. You've got almost nothing... It's time to Just Get Started!

details.title: The Hero of a Thousand Typos
details.description:
You stare blankly at the screen as the clock strikes midnight. The publisher wants to see the novel in 5 minutes and your editor is definitely not going to let you forget about that. I guess it's time to... Just Get Started!
But if that wasn't bad enough, your nephew hacked your computer to force you to play his dumb web games... And it's almost like they have a psychic effect on your editor? He seems to get really aggressive and stringent when you fail them. And hands you cool bonus topics that boost sales when you win. Better get good, I suppose!

Writing:
When you write, try to write using proper grammar and pacing. That means capitalizing every first letter, using actual REAL words, using varied words, avoiding run-on sentences, and avoiding excessive mini sentences will all contribute to your score. Now, the real jackpot lies in the bonus words embedded in the topics your editor hands you when you win a minigame. Try to feel out the words... you'll know you've hit one when that rainbow BONUS effect pops up. 

Minigames:
React quickly, and stay on your toes! These dastardly web games can be rather tricky, and winning them is key to gaining bonus themes. And losing will seriously tick off the editor,  causing him to demand some strange request from you... like getting rid of the letter "m" or not using the word "the".

(Note: These affect your written text retroactively too! Deleting those will stave off the editor's rage though, so do try to do so!)
Leaderboard:
Check out the leaderboard! Unfortunately, it currently only displays the title of your story and your score. If you'd like to keep your story, try copy and pasting it or taking a screenshot! Leave it in the comments if you'd like!

S Rank - 1200 points

A rank: 1000 points

B rank: 500 points

C rank: 300 points

D rank: 0 points

F rank: Negative points

Credits:
Rooftoppe - Programming and Music

MAJIMASWIFE - Art

Fur Elise by Ludwig van Beethoven

game.title: Code Crusader
game.short_text: Fight the evil errors by coding, and work your way to becoming a functional program!

details.title: Code Crusader
details.description:
Learn to code in GDScript* in this top-down puzzle dungeon crawler, in which you can only control the character by writing code. Learn basic programming concepts and overcome the evil errors along the way! 
You can skip levels if you want, just type this at the start of your file
goto_level(1) # takes you to level 1, etcget_tree().root.get_child(0).current_level+1 # gets current level for use with goto_level
Game Design; Zshandi Krahn and Lachlann MacUisdin
Programming; Zshandi Krahn and Lachlann MacUisdin
Art: Lachlann MacUisdin
Music: Brendan Marney

*There are several differences with GDScript, including:
There is no for loop, and instead a repeat loop (we'll add for loop after the jam)The repeat loop is more convenient for simple usage, and while loop can do anything for can.Variables do not get scoped to if statements and loops (we'll add scoping later)There is no "await", however code is automatically awaited, so functions such as move will wait for the movement to completeVariables can be re-declared (we'll change this later)There is a bug which lets you put multiple function calls on the same line, but only the first one is actually executed (no error)There are no functions, but we plan to add this in the future
Known Issues:
FIXED: The check_move function does not include enemies and the floppy disk. This makes some of the later levels unplayable. We have a fix for this and may upload an external build for that.You can place multiple function calls on a single line without errors, but it will only run the first oneAutocomplete doesn't work great, and at some points may insert quotes with the optionsAlthough it's fun to do, we'll probably need to limit what is accessible so the game can't be broken from the code, especially if incorporate multiplayer or high scores
Possible Future Additions:
Coding:
For loopsFunctionsMay completely revamp the code execution to execute everything as a proper GDScript fileBreakpointsImmediate code line execution mode (like a command prompt)
Game Play:
Items, switches, and other interactive objectsMore variety of enemiesMore levelsScoring, saving and loading progressTutorial type help pop-ups (as opposed to current "go read the docs" approach)Eventually, maybe we'll add some form of multiplayer

game.title: Sokobegan
game.short_text: Made for the Shovel Jam 2025

details.title: Sokobegan
details.description:
Small game made for the 2025 Inbound Shovel game jam with the theme "Just Get Started". Click on a character to plan out their moves in a confined area, before watching as they repeat those actions in a loop. Coordinate multiple characters by recording paths for each one, and timing their loops to push boxes and reach targets in the shortest turns possible.IMPORTANT:
Some of these levels (especially the later ones) can be very difficult. Don't be afraid to skip levels if you are stuck or "spoil" solutions. I recommend skipping levels first, then coming back later to see the solution. The last level (15) is ridiculous and I don't expect anyone to legitimately solve it. Spoiling earlier levels can also help you with logic for later ones, so if a level seems impossible try going back to levels you skipped.Controls:
WASD or Arrow keys or Swipe (touch screen and mouse) to move
Space -> StepZ -> UndoC -> RedoX -> SkipH -> Spoil
Your progress is saved, so feel free to come back later!
